name: Agent S
description: Agent S is an open-source autonomous agent framework by Simular AI for GUI desktop automation. Uses Experience-Augmented Hierarchical Planning with a Manager-Worker architecture. Achieves ~34.5% on OSWorld benchmark. Supports macOS, Linux, Windows with multiple LLM backends.
oneLiner: Open-source GUI computer-use agent with hierarchical planning for autonomous desktop task execution.
funding: Seed-stage, Simular AI (UC San Diego spinout). <$5M estimated.
pricing: Open source (Apache 2.0, free). Users pay only LLM API costs.

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Code-first Python. Clone repo, install deps (Python 3.10+, platform accessibility libs), configure LLM keys. Define tasks as natural language. Agent runs via CLI, captures screenshots, interacts with desktop GUI. Results in logged trajectories.
strengths: Novel hierarchical planning advances GUI agent capabilities
Works with any desktop app — no app-specific integration
Self-experience retrieval enables learning from past tasks
weaknesses: No enterprise features (auth, RBAC, audit, compliance)
GUI interaction is slow and fragile to UI changes
No HITL controls or kill switches
Vision model API costs are high
notableFeatures: Accessibility tree integration alongside visual screenshots
OSWorld benchmark leader (first >30% on full test)

recentDevelopments: 2025 Q1: Agent S v2 paper with experience-augmented hierarchical planning
2024 Q4: ~34.5% OSWorld success rate, surpassing previous best
2024 Q3: Initial release with Manager-Worker architecture

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Code-first Python. Clone repo, install deps, configure keys. 30+ min to first run.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Screenshot replay of trajectories. No interactive debugger or testing framework.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: GitHub README + research paper. Limited tutorials. Steep learning curve.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Full re-execution per iteration (minutes per run). No hot-reload.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Manager-Worker is multi-agent but fixed two-tier pattern only.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: No HITL design. Agent operates independently.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: No team features. Single-developer research tool.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Benchmark configurations as informal templates. No marketplace.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: No pre-built connectors. All interaction through GUI.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: Python API. No MCP, REST API, or webhooks.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: No auth management. Env var API keys only.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Web knowledge retrieval + self-experience retrieval. No enterprise RAG.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: No guardrails. Agent has full GUI control with no limits.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: No approval mechanism. Fully autonomous.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Screenshot trajectory logging. No structured audit or compliance.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: No access control. Single-user, single-machine.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Local desktop only. Requires display server for GUI. No cloud or container deployment.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Single-instance, single-session. No horizontal scaling.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: No monitoring dashboard. Basic file-based logging.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: ~3.8K GitHub stars. Small research community. Conference presence.

scoringRationale: Agent S scores 34/100. Research tool, not an enterprise platform. Builder Experience (7) requires Python expertise. Deployment (4) single-machine only. Integration (7) GUI-only. Governance (4) no controls. Collaboration (7) Manager-Worker pattern. Ecosystem (5) early-stage research.

walkthrough.title: How Agent S Works: Autonomous Desktop GUI Control

steps.title: Set Up the Environment
steps.description: Clone the repo, install Python 3.10+ dependencies, and configure platform-specific accessibility libraries (macOS Accessibility API, Linux AT-SPI, Windows UI Automation). Set your LLM API key (GPT-4o, Claude, or open-source models).

steps.title: Define a Task in Natural Language
steps.description: Describe what you want the agent to do: 'Open the spreadsheet, copy Q3 revenue figures, create a chart in Slides, and email the presentation to the team.' The agent decomposes this into hierarchical sub-tasks.

steps.title: Manager-Worker Planning
steps.description: The Manager agent creates a high-level plan using Experience-Augmented Hierarchical Planning. It retrieves relevant past trajectories and web knowledge to inform the strategy. Worker agents execute individual UI actions.

steps.title: Autonomous GUI Interaction
steps.description: Worker agents capture screenshots, parse accessibility trees, and interact with desktop applications — clicking buttons, typing text, navigating menus. Each action is logged as a trajectory for future learning.

steps.title: Results and Trajectory Logging
steps.description: The agent reports task completion status with screenshots of the final state. All trajectories are stored for self-experience retrieval, improving performance on similar future tasks. Evaluate on OSWorld benchmark (~34.5%).
